items.title: Smelter left in limbo after consortium member effectively pulls out of bid

children.content: Workers at Tasmania's troubled Liberty Bell Bay smelter have been told administrators have until Monday to secure alternative funding, after a member of the preferred consortium bidding to take over the manganese smelter effectively pulled out.

gallery.altText: A blue sign with a flame on it and the word 'LIBERTY' underneath, on the side of the road.

thumbnail.altText: A blue sign with a flame on it and the word 'LIBERTY' underneath, on the side of the road.

items.title: EPA lifts processing ban for recycling facility to reduce fire risk

children.content: A ban on metal shredding and processing is lifted for a northern Tasmanian facility following lead dust contamination.

image.altText: A blue crane lifts a large white container onto a pile of scrap metal.

items.title: New line of hope in discovering the Nannup Four

children.content: The vice-president of a search team that solved two high-profile missing persons cases in Tasmania turns his attention to the Nannup Four case after it was featured in the Expanse podcast.

image.altText: A photo of two men, a woman and a girl with a photo of bushland in the background.

items.title: Greens say premier's answers about legal fees 'paint a far worse picture'

children.content: Tasmanian Premier Jeremy Rockliff has provided answers to a series of questions about former minister Madeleine Ogilvie's legal fees matter, but the Greens say they "paint a far worse picture of this shameful saga and his role in it".

articles.title: What happens next for Madeleine Ogilvie's legal fee saga?

children.content: It's been two weeks since Madeleine Ogilvie resigned from Tasmanian cabinet, and the saga is reaching a tipping point. What could happen when parliament returns?

image.altText: Tasmanian parliament building from outside.

articles.title: Tasmanian premier links minister's court action to Integrity Commission

children.content: Premier Jeremy Rockliff links comments made by the boss of the state's Integrity Commission to a question about former minister Madeleine Ogilvie's Supreme Court action.

image.altText: Woman sitting in a green chair

gallery.altText: Jeremy Rockliff walks into a room at Government House, surrounded by three other men wearing suits.

thumbnail.altText: Jeremy Rockliff walks into a room at Government House, surrounded by three other men wearing suits.

items.title: Police investigating shed fire where man's body was found

children.content: A man's body has been found following a shed fire in southern Tasmania's Richmond, which police say appears to have been deliberately lit.

image.altText: street signs on a power pole

items.title: $100k payout after theft of iconic Tasmanian wool trophy

children.content: The trophy features a sculpture that includes a 24-carat gold merino sheep and a weaving loom made of silver and platinum, with its theft last year captured on CCTV.

image.altText: A thief in a light-coloured hoodie holds a hammer and smashes a glass cabinet.

items.title: 'Irreversible damage': Aboriginal tools covered by new path

children.content: A new walking and cycling path has been built over what an Aboriginal organisation describes as a "significant" cultural site in northern Tasmania — and it didn't require a permit.

gallery.altText: Reuben West and Joel Williams wear khaki shirts and stand in the bush.
A stand of gum trees behind a cleared area with a road winding past.
A man in a khaki shirt walk up a path in the bush

thumbnail.altText: Reuben West and Joel Williams wear khaki shirts and stand in the bush.
A stand of gum trees behind a cleared area with a road winding past.
A man in a khaki shirt walk up a path in the bush

avatar.alt: A woman with straight, fair, shoulder-length hair smiles. She wears a black top.

items.title: TassieCast: Is this really Tasmania's 'most transparent' government?

children.content: Transparency and trust in government are cornerstones of any successful democracy. So how does it feel not knowing why almost half a million dollars of taxpayers money has so far been spent on government MPs' legal bills? Airlie Ward talks with state political reporter Josh Duggan.

items.title: Australian sprinter Jemma Stapleton dies on overseas family holiday

children.content: The Australian athletics world is in mourning after the death of the 25-year-old Stawell Gift finalist.

gallery.altText: An athlete holds her hands up next to head and smiles.
The Australian athletics world has been left in mourning after the death of Stawell Gift finalist Jemma Stapleton.

thumbnail.altText: An athlete holds her hands up next to head and smiles.
The Australian athletics world has been left in mourning after the death of Stawell Gift finalist Jemma Stapleton.

items.title: 'Broken promise' or not, Albanese's tax decision helps One Nation

children.content: The prime minister's cavalier breaking of election promises has poured kerosene on the fire under a party that can no longer be called "minor".

image.altText: Prime minister Anthony Albanese

items.title: Notorious childcare paedophile loses appeal against non-parole period

children.content: The former childcare worker sexually abused dozens of children at centres for almost two decades.

image.altText: A headshot of Ashley Paul Griffith on a cardboard background.

items.title: Government agencies fail first hurdle under AI self-reporting policy

children.content: The ABC can reveal dozens of federal agencies missed mandatory deadlines to disclose how they are currently using AI.

image.altText: A woman's hands type on a laptop keyboard.

items.title: Air India crash plunged victims' families 'into darkness'

children.content: One year on from the fatal Air India crash that claimed 260 lives, families of victims are still waiting for a report into the investigation.

image.altText: A schreenshot of a couple from a video call.

items.title: Independence leader vanishes after changing sides to support Indonesia's government

children.content: A key Indigenous leader in West Papua appeared in a documentary criticising an Indonesian government program, but then changed sides and disappeared. Her family suspects foul play.

gallery.altText: Film poster depicting a woman in traditional Papuan clothing with a red cross in the background looking at the camera

thumbnail.altText: Film poster depicting a woman in traditional Papuan clothing with a red cross in the background looking at the camera

items.title: Amex ordered to compensate man whose ex accessed his data

children.content: Australia's privacy commissioner has threatened a complainant with legal action to prevent the full disclosure of the findings of a long-running investigation into American Express's information security, which found widespread technology failures.

image.altText: A modern office building with an American Express sign.

items.title: More couples opt for micro-weddings and elopements amid cost-of-living crisis

children.content: Melbourne couple Averill and Lloyd Johnson pulled off their dream wedding for just $3,000, compared to the average wedding cost of about $38,000.

image.altText: Groom wearing a suit and top hat and bridge wearing a black dress, holding up their middle fingers with rings on them.

items.title: 'Priceless' pieces of Cartier jewellery on display at Melbourne's NGV

children.content: Glittering tiaras and dazzling necklaces worn by Hollywood icons such as Elizabeth Taylor and Grace Kelly are featured in an exhibition at the National Gallery of Victoria in Melbourne.

gallery.altText: Elizabeth Taylor wearing a Cartier necklace and earrings at the Tropicana Hotel in Las Vegas in 1958
María Félix holding her snake Cartier necklace on her head

thumbnail.altText: Elizabeth Taylor wearing a Cartier necklace and earrings at the Tropicana Hotel in Las Vegas in 1958
María Félix holding her snake Cartier necklace on her head

items.title: How you and your superannuation came to be aboard SpaceX

children.content: If your superannuation is managed by any of Australia's big superannuation funds, chances are you're about to become a highly valued financial backer to Elon Musk.

image.altText: smoke and flames emerge from a spacex launch in texas

items.title: Roxane Gay and Channing Tatum join forces thanks to this Australian

children.content: It's possible the ball may not have got rolling on Roxane Gay and Channing Tatum's upcoming novel if it wasn't for an Australian connection. Gay will appear at Vivid Sydney in a wide-ranging discussion.

image.altText: Roxane Gay with hair in black and grey braids tied in a bun looking out a window.

items.title: Viral lookout reopens after tourists used yards as 'toilets'

children.content: Lincolns Rock shut after crowds flocked to the site to recreate a photo posted on Instagram of a K-pop star dangling her legs off the sandstone cliff.

gallery.altText: Tourists sit and stand on rock face overlooking the Blue Mountains Valley.
Line of people on a white sandstone rock.

thumbnail.altText: Tourists sit and stand on rock face overlooking the Blue Mountains Valley.
Line of people on a white sandstone rock.

items.title: Mechanism that makes Venus flytraps snap uncovered, study claims

children.content: Physicists think they have found the cellular mechanism behind a Venus flytrap's signature move, but plant scientists say the mystery isn't solved yet.

image.altText: Two Venus flytraps against a black background, one on the left is open, the one on the right is shut.

items.title: US forecasters say potentially record-breaking El Niño underway

children.content: There is a high chance this year's Pacific climate phenomenon will be among the strongest on record, which could lead to hotter temperatures across the globe.

image.altText: Storm clouds drop heavy rain in the distance on a western Queensland property

items.title: Study reveals complex links between social media use and teen wellbeing

children.content: A decade-long study following almost 1,200 Melbourne school students finds a "small increased risk" of depressive symptoms for teenagers using social media at least two hours a day.

image.altText: teens using their phones with various social media related illustrations

items.title: Bunnings facing anti-competitive case from Mitre 10 franchisee

children.content: In other finance news the ASX is trading strongly on the back of a Wall Street rally and oil prices tumble. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.title: Friday news quiz: Fake weather, a whale graveyard and James Bond going woke

children.content: Don't let your confidence be shaken or stirred by this week's test of how much you've been paying attention.

gallery.altText: Daniel Craig, as James Bond, stands next to his Aston Martin in a scene from Skyfall.

thumbnail.altText: Daniel Craig, as James Bond, stands next to his Aston Martin in a scene from Skyfall.

items.title: Driver spends $160 rego rebate on pothole-blown tyre

children.content: Motorists in Victoria's south-west say they are spending their car rego rebate on blown tyres following damage caused by potholes.

image.altText: a man in a navy jumper stands in front of a tyre

items.title: Sydney woman's remains believed found eight years after alleged murder

children.content: Nadire Sensoy disappeared from Sydney's west in 2018, with her disappearance triggering a police reward and coronial inquest.

image.altText: A woman smiling and wearing a blue shirt.

items.title: Creditors of NT mine with $360m debt criticise lack of oversight

children.content: In the wake of Nathan River Resources entering into voluntary administration, creditors have criticised the NT government for its lack of oversight of the struggling company. ASIC documents show the mining company owes more than $360 million.

image.altText: A man standing with his arms folded, in front of several long metal pipes in an outdoor work yard.

items.title: Estranged son charged with alleged kidnapping murder of his mother

children.content: Family members told police they last heard from the woman on Monday, when she told them she was visiting a relative at a reserve in Sydney's north-west.

image.altText: Police tape at a crime scene

items.title: Aboriginal people were kicked off this land. Their descendant has bought it back

children.content: Gunditjmara man Craig Molyneux buys back land that was stolen from his ancestors and infuses the property's old church with his Indigenous culture.

gallery.altText: Ros and Craig Molyneux stand out the front of their new house, that was previously a church.
A sign for the St Philips Church  Of England
The entryway of a house with images and artwork on the walls.

thumbnail.altText: Ros and Craig Molyneux stand out the front of their new house, that was previously a church.
A sign for the St Philips Church  Of England
The entryway of a house with images and artwork on the walls.

items.title: Thai king's eldest daughter dies after long illness

children.content: Princess Bajrakitiyabha Narendira Debyavati, who had spent the past three and a half years in a coma due to a heart condition, is being remembered for her efforts to improve the livelihoods of female prisoners and her diplomatic career.

image.altText: A formally dressed middle-aged Thai woman smiles as she leans down to greet members of a crowd.

items.title: Three decades later, 18 soldiers lost in a training crash are not forgotten

children.content: During counterterrorism training in 1996, two Black Hawk helicopters collided. Eighteen men were killed, making it Australia's worst peacetime military aviation disaster.

image.altText: Graphic showing a helicopter, crash remnants, and tributes

items.title: Major PFAS class action could lead to multi-billion dollar claim figure

children.content: Hundreds of businesses and organisations are part of a major Victorian Supreme Court class action against chemical manufacturer 3M.

image.altText: An industrial plant in the Melbourne suburb of Altona.

items.title: International students face a double financial blow

children.content: When Jolie Cao chose to do her undergraduate university degree in Australia, she knew it would be expensive. But an additional financial pressure has made things "really hard".

image.altText: A woman with black hair in a puffer jacket smiling to camera.

items.title: Canberra's only water park is up for sale. Here's why that's good news

children.content: The site will not undergo redevelopment or demolition before its sale and locals say it is the result they fought for.

image.altText: Three tower waterslides, all in a significant state of disrepair, surrounded by gum trees.
